title: Daily Routines
shortSummary: Describe your day from morning to night using common reflexive and daily verbs.
culturalNote: The Italian day has its own rhythm. Breakfast (la colazione) is light, usually a coffee and a pastry standing at the bar. The main meal is traditionally lunch (il pranzo), though city life is shifting that to dinner (la cena), which is eaten late, often after eight. Many daily-routine actions are reflexive verbs in Italian: you 'wake yourself', 'wash yourself', and 'dress yourself'. The evening passeggiata, a relaxed stroll before dinner, remains a beloved daily habit in towns across the country.

grammar.explanation: Many routine verbs are reflexive in Italian and need a pronoun: svegliarsi (to wake up), alzarsi (to get up), lavarsi (to wash). The pronoun matches the subject: mi sveglio, ti svegli, si sveglia.
grammar.commonMistake: Dropping the reflexive pronoun. Sveglio alone means 'I wake (someone)', not 'I wake up'.

grammar.explanation: To say at what time something happens, use a plus the hour, which contracts to alle (or all' before una). Alle sette means 'at seven', all'una means 'at one'.
grammar.commonMistake: Forgetting to contract a + le into alle when stating a time.

dialogue.setup: Luca tells his friend Marta about his weekday.

lines.speaker: Marta
lines.native: A che ora ti svegli di solito?
lines.english: What time do you usually wake up?

lines.speaker: Luca
lines.native: Mi sveglio alle sei e mezza.
lines.english: I wake up at half past six.

lines.speaker: Luca
lines.native: Faccio colazione e vado al lavoro.
lines.english: I have breakfast and go to work.

lines.speaker: Marta
lines.native: A che ora torni a casa?
lines.english: What time do you come home?

lines.speaker: Luca
lines.native: Verso le sei, poi ceno alle otto.
lines.english: Around six, then I have dinner at eight.

quiz.question: How do you say 'I wake up at seven' in Italian?
quiz.explanation: Svegliarsi is reflexive, so it needs mi.

quiz.question: Why does 'mi alzo' include 'mi'?
quiz.explanation: Reflexive verbs need a matching pronoun like mi.

quiz.question: Arrange: dormire / vado / a
quiz.explanation: Vado a dormire means 'I go to sleep'.
